explanation: Uses the exact executable name and argument pattern the rule looks for, but changes the working directory/path context so the literal command-line still contains 'connectionStrings' and ' -pdf' while potentially evading simple Image/OriginalFileName matches that rely on a default path; an attacker can also copy aspnet_regiis.exe to a non-standard location or rename it and invoke it via .NET reflection or rundll32 to avoid the Image/endswith and OriginalFileName filters entirely.

Invokes the same decryption functionality via rundll32 so the Image is rundll32.exe (not aspnet_regiis.exe), bypassing the Image|endswith and OriginalFileName filters while still performing the decryption; the command-line may still contain the keywords, but the process ancestry and image name no longer match the rule.

Uses PowerShell to load aspnet_regiis.exe as an assembly and invoke decryption routines directly, avoiding creation of an aspnet_regiis.exe process entirely; the command-line does not contain the literal ' -pdf' pattern, bypassing the CommandLine|contains|all condition.
